title: acer pectinatum subsp. pectinatum
intro: Acer pectinatum subsp. pectinatum, also known as the Himalayan maple or Amur maple, is a subspecies of deciduous maple tree native to East Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it useful for treating various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. The health benefits of Acer pectinatum subsp. pectinatum include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
